title: Smoky Creamy Australian Barramundi Grill
description: A fragrant grilled barramundi fillet finished in a silky, lowsodium cream sauce perfumed with cardamom and smoked paprika, perfect for a cozy Aussie dinner.
instructions: Preheat a grill or grill pan to medium‑high heat. Brush the grill grates with a little olive oil to prevent sticking.
In a small bowl, combine smoked paprika, ground cardamom, sea salt, and black pepper. Pat the barramundi fillets dry with paper towels, then rub the spice mix evenly over both sides.
Place the fillets skin‑side down on the grill and sear for 3–4 minutes until bright golden brown and slightly crispy.
Flip the fillets and brush the tops with a thin layer of olive oil. Grill for another 3–4 minutes, or until the fish flakes easily when tested with a fork.
While the fish is grilling, pour the low‑sodium fish stock into a small saucepan over medium heat and bring to a light simmer.
Add the heavy cream, lemon zest, and lemon juice to the simmering stock, stirring to combine.
Reduce the heat to low and let the sauce thicken slightly, about 2–3 minutes. Stir in the chopped dill, and season with an additional pinch of sea salt if needed.
Remove the fillets from the grill and let them rest for a minute. Place each fillet on a serving plate and spoon the creamy cardamom‑lemon sauce over the top.
Dot each fillet with a small pat of unsalted butter, allowing it to melt into the sauce for extra richness.
Serve immediately with a side of lightly sautéed spinach or a fresh green salad for a balanced meal.
